name: tinja
title: tinja

commands.name: tinja

arguments.description: set the path for a config file to be read
arguments.raw: --config string          set the path for a config file to be read

arguments.description: enable scanning for Client-Side Template Injections using a headless browser
arguments.raw: --csti                   enable scanning for Client-Side Template Injections using a headless browser

arguments.description: escape HTML special chars in the JSON report
arguments.raw: --escapereport           escape HTML special chars in the JSON report

arguments.description: how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the preceding chars of a body reflection point (default 30)
arguments.raw: --precedinglength int    how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the preceding chars of a body reflection point (default 30)

arguments.description: set the path for the certificate of the proxy
arguments.raw: --proxycertpath string   set the path for the certificate of the proxy

arguments.description: set the URL of the proxy
arguments.raw: --proxyurl string        set the URL of the proxy

arguments.description: number of requests per seconds. 0 is infinite (default 0)
arguments.raw: -r, --ratelimit float        number of requests per seconds. 0 is infinite (default 0)

arguments.description: set the path for a report to be generated
arguments.raw: --reportpath string      set the path for a report to be generated

arguments.description: how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the subsequent chars of a body reflection point (default 30)
arguments.raw: --subsequentlength int   how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the subsequent chars of a body reflection point (default 30)
--testheaders strings    headers to test. E.g. --testheaders Host,Origin,X-Forwarded-For

arguments.description: set chrome as user-agent. Default user-agent is 'TInjA v1.2.0'
arguments.raw: --useragentchrome        set chrome as user-agent. Default user-agent is 'TInjA v1.2.0'

arguments.description: verbosity of the output. 0 = quiet, 1 = default, 2 = verbose (default 1)
arguments.raw: -v, --verbosity int          verbosity of the output. 0 = quiet, 1 = default, 2 = verbose (default 1)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# tinja -h
 
 __/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\__/\\\\\\\\\\\______________________________/\\\\\\\\\____        
_\///////\\\/////__\/////\\\///______________________/\\\___/\\\\\\\\\\\\\__       
 _______\/\\\___________\/\\\________________________\///___/\\\/////////\\\_      
  _______\/\\\___________\/\\\______/\\/\\\\\\_________/\\\_\/\\\_______\/\\\_     
   _______\/\\\___________\/\\\_____\/\\\////\\\_______\/\\\_\/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\_    
    _______\/\\\___________\/\\\_____\/\\\__\//\\\______\/\\\_\/\\\/////////\\\_   
     _______\/\\\___________\/\\\_____\/\\\___\/\\\__/\\_\/\\\_\/\\\_______\/\\\_  
      _______\/\\\________/\\\\\\\\\\\_\/\\\___\/\\\_\//\\\\\\__\/\\\_______\/\\\_ 
       _______\///________\///////////__\///____\///___\//////___\///________\///__
 
 the Template INJection Analyzer. (v1.2.0)
 
 Published by Hackmanit under http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
 Author: Maximilian Hildebrand
 Repository: https://github.com/Hackmanit/TInjA
 
 Usage:
 tinja [flags]
 tinja [command]
 
 Available Commands:
 help        Help about any command
 jsonl       Scan using a JSONL file
 raw         Scan using a Raw file
 url         Scan a single or multiple URLs
 
 Flags:
     --config string          set the path for a config file to be read
 -c, --cookie strings         add custom cookie(s)
     --csti                   enable scanning for Client-Side Template Injections using a headless browser
     --escapereport           escape HTML special chars in the JSON report
 -H, --header strings         add custom header(s)
 -h, --help                   help for tinja
     --precedinglength int    how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the preceding chars of a body reflection point (default 30)
     --proxycertpath string   set the path for the certificate of the proxy
     --proxyurl string        set the URL of the proxy
 -r, --ratelimit float        number of requests per seconds. 0 is infinite (default 0)
     --reportpath string      set the path for a report to be generated
     --subsequentlength int   how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the subsequent chars of a body reflection point (default 30)
     --testheaders strings    headers to test. E.g. --testheaders Host,Origin,X-Forwarded-For
     --timeout int            seconds until timeout (default 15)
     --useragentchrome        set chrome as user-agent. Default user-agent is 'TInjA v1.2.0'
 -v, --verbosity int          verbosity of the output. 0 = quiet, 1 = default, 2 = verbose (default 1)
     --version                version for tinja
 
 Use "tinja [command] --help" for more information about a command.

Available Commands:

  raw         Scan using a Raw file

Flags:
      --config string          set the path for a config file to be read
  -c, --cookie strings         add custom cookie(s)
      --csti                   enable scanning for Client-Side Template Injections using a headless browser

      --proxycertpath string   set the path for the certificate of the proxy
      --proxyurl string        set the URL of the proxy
  -r, --ratelimit float        number of requests per seconds. 0 is infinite (default 0)
      --reportpath string      set the path for a report to be generated
      --subsequentlength int   how many chars shall be memorized, when getting the subsequent chars of a body reflection point (default 30)
      --testheaders strings    headers to test. E.g. --testheaders Host,Origin,X-Forwarded-For
      --timeout int            seconds until timeout (default 15)
      --useragentchrome        set chrome as user-agent. Default user-agent is 'TInjA v1.2.0'
  -v, --verbosity int          verbosity of the output. 0 = quiet, 1 = default, 2 = verbose (default 1)

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=562da170a35c5d7073149e657016c923e04bde94, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
CLI tool for testing web pages for template injection
TInjA is a CLI tool for testing web pages for template injection
vulnerabilities and supports 44 of the most relevant template engines for eight
different programming languages.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tinja because ### tinja.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tinja with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tinja --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### tinja.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install tinja
